title: 以下について精査せよ。 Supabase <noreply@supabase.com> 8:49 (6 分前) To masa.stage1 Hello, Impressive growth with your Supabase organization cursorvers (ID: hwfgxgzoamzxnjutjril)! It’s doing so well that it breezed through your plan’s quota. You can continue using Supabase at your current usage for this billing...
summary_text: **Recommendation** - Current cached overage cost: `1,278.13 GB × $0.03/GB = $38.34` so far this cycle. - Simple end-of-cycle exposure if the same pace continues: billing cycle is `2026-03-27` to `2026-04-27` `(~31 days)`, and on `2026-04-09` you are about `13 days` in. That implies roughly `98.3 GB/day` of cached overage, or about `3,048 GB` total overage by cycle end. At `$0.03/GB`, that is about `+$91.44` total overage for the cycle, or about `+$53.10` more from now to cycle end. - Uncached egress is negligible here: `1.646 GB / 250 GB`, so it is not the decision driver. **Spend Cap Criteria** - Keep `Spend Cap` **ON** if you want hard cost containment and can tolerate possible restriction behavior while you fix the traffic source. Supabase documents that with Spend Cap on, over-quota usage is disallowed until the next cycle. - Turn `Spend Cap` **OFF** only if uninterrupted service is more important than variable cost, and you are comfortable absorbing about `$91` of cached egress overage this cycle at the current pace. - Because the grace period lasts until `2026-05-09`, the practical cutoff is: if you can get cached egress back under `250 GB` before then, leave it ON; if not, and availability matters more than the incremental cost, switch it OFF. Sources: [Manage Egress usage](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/platform/manage-your-usage/egress), [Control your costs](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/platform/spend-cap), [Billing FAQ](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/platform/billing-faq)
